root_text.an8.26:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati jīvakambavane.
root_text.an8.26:1.2: Atha kho jīvako komārabhacco yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho jīvako komārabhacco bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.26:1.4: “Yato kho, jīvaka, buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti;
root_text.an8.26:2.2: “Yato kho, jīvaka, upāsako pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti …pe… surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti;
root_text.an8.26:2.3: ettāvatā kho, jīvaka, upāsako sīlavā hotī”ti.
root_text.an8.26:3.1: “Kittāvatā pana, bhante, upāsako attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāyā”ti?
root_text.an8.26:3.2: “Yato kho, jīvaka, upāsako attanāva saddhāsampanno hoti, no paraṁ saddhāsampadāya samādapeti …pe…
root_text.an8.26:3.3: attanāva atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ dhammānudhammappaṭipattiyā samādapeti.
root_text.an8.26:3.4: Ettāvatā kho, jīvaka, upāsako attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāyā”ti.
root_text.an8.26:4.1: “Kittāvatā pana, bhante, upāsako attahitāya ca paṭipanno hoti parahitāya cā”ti?
root_text.an8.26:4.2: “Yato kho, jīvaka, upāsako attanā ca saddhāsampanno hoti, parañca saddhāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.3: attanā ca sīlasampanno hoti, parañca sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.4: attanā ca cāgasampanno hoti, parañca cāgasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.5: attanā ca bhikkhūnaṁ dassanakāmo hoti, parañca bhikkhūnaṁ dassane samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.6: attanā ca saddhammaṁ sotukāmo hoti, parañca saddhammassavane samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.7: attanā ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti, parañca dhammadhāraṇāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.8: attanā ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti, parañca atthūpaparikkhāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.26:4.9: attanā ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti, parañca dhammānudhammappaṭipattiyā samādapeti.
root_text.an8.26:4.10: Ettāvatā kho, jīvaka, upāsako attahitāya ca paṭipanno hoti parahitāya cā”ti.

translation_text.an8.26:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha in Jīvaka’s Mango Grove.
translation_text.an8.26:1.2: Then Jīvaka Komārabhacca went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an8.26:1.3: “Sir, how is a lay follower defined?”
translation_text.an8.26:1.4: “Jīvaka, when you’ve gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha,
translation_text.an8.26:1.5: you’re considered to be a lay follower.”
translation_text.an8.26:2.1: “But how is an ethical lay follower defined?”
translation_text.an8.26:2.2: “When a lay follower doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants,
translation_text.an8.26:2.3: they’re considered to be an ethical lay follower.”
translation_text.an8.26:3.1: “But how do we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit themselves, not others?”
translation_text.an8.26:3.2: “A lay follower is accomplished in faith, but doesn’t encourage others to do the same. They’re accomplished in ethical conduct … they’re accomplished in generosity … they like to see the mendicants … they like to hear the true teaching … they memorize the teachings … they examine the meaning …
translation_text.an8.26:3.3: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching, but they don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:3.4: That’s how we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit themselves, not others.”
translation_text.an8.26:4.1: “But how do we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit both themselves and others?”
translation_text.an8.26:4.2: “A lay follower is accomplished in faith and encourages others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.3: They’re accomplished in ethical conduct and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.4: They’re accomplished in generosity and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.5: They like to see the mendicants and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.6: They like to hear the true teaching and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.7: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.8: They examine the meaning of the teachings they’ve memorized and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.9: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching and they encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.26:4.10: That’s how we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit both themselves and others.”
